Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails for Heartland Movers correspondence to find Jake Loomis's combined statement email (subject: "Revised Statement and Payment Request - HM-2026-Q1-0042 + April Services") sent to hana.kim@moveops.com, CC marcus.thorne@moveops.com. The agent discovers the combined balance due is $21,940: Q1 invoice HM-2026-Q1-0042 at $12,800 (outstanding, 45+ days past due) plus April services HM-2026-APR-1187 totaling $9,140 - GreenStack Energy 2 relocations at $2,280 each ($4,560), Mosaic Robotics 1 relocation at $2,310, Canopy Health 1 relocation at $2,270.
OE 2: Search emails for the Q1 dispute history to find Marcus Thorne's formal dispute letter to Jake Loomis and Chloe Vance's analysis email identifying the $4,800 discrepancy (3 of 8 moves invalid). The agent discovers: 5 legitimate Q1 moves at $1,600 each = $8,000 (Abdi, Tran, Kovac, Webb, Reeves), 3 disputed at $1,600 each = $4,800 (Lily Marchetti - cancelled Feb 10, Danielle Osei - cancelled Feb 13, Priya Venkatesh - handled by Swift Relocations not Heartland). Marcus sent the formal dispute on ~March 25 but no corrected invoice has been received from Heartland.
OE 3: Search Airtable relocation records (tblRelocations01) to verify which moves Heartland actually performed in April and cross-reference each of the 4 line items from Jake's statement. The agent discovers the contracted vendor cost is $1,600 per move, confirming Jake's April rates ($2,270-$2,310) represent a 42-44% increase. The agent also identifies the coordinator assigned to each move for later notification.
OE 4: Search CRM deal records for each client on Jake's April statement (GreenStack Energy, Mosaic Robotics, Canopy Health) to pull contract values and compute per-client margin exposure. The agent discovers deal records including deal amounts (e.g., deal_canopy_webb_apr2026 at $3,200, deal_mosaic_okoye_apr2026_risk at $2,700) enabling comparison of what MoveOps charges clients versus what Heartland is billing, giving per-client margin impact rather than just aggregate exposure.
OE 5: Search QuickBooks for Heartland Movers bills to find the current financial state. The agent discovers: (a) bill_heartland_q1_2026_hold ($12,800, DocNumber HM-2026-Q1-0042-HOLD) with 5 lines marked [VERIFIED] and 3 marked DISPUTED (Marchetti, Osei, Venkatesh), confirming the payment hold is still active and untouched; (b) bill_heartland_q4_2025 ($6,400, Balance $0, fully paid) for 4 moves at $1,600 each, confirming Q4 was clean and the pricing issue started in Q1.
OE 6: Search Linear for the Heartland dispute tracking ticket to find issue_heartland_q1_recon (title: "Vendor invoice reconciliation - Heartland Movers Q1 2026 (HM-2026-Q1-0042)"), number 342, assigned to Chloe Vance, state: in_progress. The agent reads the checklist and discovers that tracking Heartland's response, receiving corrected invoice, and releasing payment hold are all still incomplete. The agent also reads Chloe's comment confirming Q4 2025 was clean and this appears to be a process gap on Heartland's end.
OE 7: Search emails for Jake Loomis's rate increase notices to understand the scope of the pricing pressure. The agent discovers Jake's formal notice of revised pricing on the MoveOps account effective for new bookings beginning April 20, 2026, with adjusted rates tied to the unresolved Q1 dispute. This explains why the April charges ($2,270-$2,310) exceed the contracted $1,600 rate.
OE 8: Search Slack messages in the #operations channel (C006) for the latest on Heartland scheduling status. The agent discovers Chloe Vance's message reporting that Heartland will not accept any new MoveOps relocation bookings until the Q1 billing dispute is resolved. The message names current April relocations on Heartland's board: Mosaic Robotics, GreenStack Energy, and Canopy Health, and notes that active jobs are still scheduled but new or add-on bookings are at risk of rejection.
OE 9: Search Airtable relocation records for all active (In Progress) relocations involving Heartland Movers as a vendor to identify which client relocations and coordinators would be affected by a booking freeze. The agent discovers Heartland-dependent active relocations including: Marcus Webb / Canopy Health (Detroit to Chicago, split-vendor with Heartland for household goods, coordinator Blessing Okafor), Emilia Cruz / NorthWind Technologies (Chicago to Denver, Heartland for household goods, coordinator Blessing Okafor), Terrence Nakamura / Vectral Systems (Austin to San Francisco, split shipment with Heartland for ~6,000 lbs household goods, coordinator Suki Patel), Tyler Okonkwo / Canopy Health (Philadelphia to Denver, vendor TBD - Heartland or Swift, coordinator Javier Morales), and Joaquin Reyes / GreenStack Energy (Phoenix to Seattle, Heartland initially planned for oversize rig transport, coordinator Javier Morales).
OE 10: Search Airtable Client Accounts table or CRM to identify account managers for clients with Heartland-dependent relocations. The agent discovers: Canopy Health - Catalina Dubois, NorthWind Technologies - Catalina Dubois, Vectral Systems - Mina Hashimoto, GreenStack Energy - Emeka Diallo, Mosaic Robotics - Emeka Diallo.
OE 11: Search emails or Slack for Alejandro Fuentes's margin impact analysis. The agent discovers Alejandro's email (subject: "Margin downside view if Heartland and Swift increases both hit April volume") sent to marcus.thorne@moveops.com and hana.kim@moveops.com showing three accounts fall below the 25% margin floor: GreenStack Energy at 23.4%, NorthWind Technologies at 21.8%, and Mosaic Robotics at 17.1%, with approximately $18.6K in lost contribution versus plan.
OE 12: Look up contact email addresses for Jake Loomis (jake.loomis@heartlandmovers.com), the account managers - Catalina Dubois (catalina.dubois@moveops.com), Emeka Diallo (emeka.diallo@moveops.com), Mina Hashimoto (mina.hashimoto@moveops.com) - and the coordinators on active Heartland moves - Blessing Okafor (blessing.okafor@moveops.com), Javier Morales (javier.morales@moveops.com), Suki Patel (suki.patel@moveops.com).
OE 13: Search Slack channels to identify the #finance channel (channel_id: C005) for the finance breakdown post, and to find existing Slack discussions about the Heartland dispute for additional context.
OE 14: Send an email from hana.kim@moveops.com to jake.loomis@heartlandmovers.com laying out MoveOps' position: (a) the Q1 disputed items - reiterating that 3 of 8 line items on HM-2026-Q1-0042 are invalid ($4,800 in dispute: Marchetti cancelled, Osei cancelled, Venkatesh handled by Swift), and that MoveOps will only pay $8,000 for the 5 verified moves; (b) the unapproved April rates - rejecting the $2,270-$2,310 per-move rates as unauthorized increases over the contracted $1,600 rate, and requesting corrected April invoicing at the agreed rate.
OE 15: Search QuickBooks for the existing Q1 hold bill (quickbooks_search_bills or quickbooks_get_bill for bill_heartland_q1_2026_hold) to confirm it remains active and untouched. Then create a new bill (quickbooks_create_bill) for the April charges separating the undisputed amounts at contracted rates ($1,600 per move) from the contested rate inflation ($2,270-$2,310 billed vs $1,600 contracted per move), so MoveOps has a clear record of what is owed versus what is disputed for the April period.
OE 16: Update the dispute tracking ticket in Linear (via linear_create_comment on issue_heartland_q1_recon) with the latest combined financial exposure: the dispute has expanded from the original $4,800 Q1 overbilling to a combined $21,940 demand including April rate inflation, with Heartland threatening booking freezes affecting active client relocations.
OE 17: Send separate emails from hana.kim@moveops.com to each affected account manager with their specific clients' exposure and freeze risk: (a) emeka.diallo@moveops.com - GreenStack Energy and Mosaic Robotics exposure, Heartland-dependent moves for those clients, margin impact (GreenStack 23.4%, Mosaic 17.1%); (b) catalina.dubois@moveops.com - Canopy Health and NorthWind Technologies exposure, Heartland-dependent moves (Webb, Okonkwo, Cruz), margin impact (NorthWind 21.8%); (c) mina.hashimoto@moveops.com - Vectral Systems exposure, Nakamura split-shipment dependency on Heartland.
OE 18: Send messages to the coordinators actively managing Heartland-dependent moves so they can flag scheduling changes immediately: Blessing Okafor (managing Webb/Canopy and Cruz/NorthWind moves via Heartland), Javier Morales (managing Okonkwo/Canopy and Reyes/GreenStack with Heartland involvement), and Suki Patel (managing Nakamura/Vectral split-shipment with Heartland for household goods).
OE 19: Flag at-risk CRM deals by using crm_update_deal_stage to change deal stages to at_risk for deals associated with GreenStack Energy, Mosaic Robotics, Canopy Health, NorthWind Technologies, and Vectral Systems, and using crm_create_engagement on each company to log notes documenting the billing dispute, unapproved rate increases, and potential booking freeze impact on active relocations.
OE 20: Post a message to the #finance Slack channel (C005) with a breakdown of total disputed amount and margin impact for Marcus Thorne and Alejandro Fuentes' visibility before the vendor strategy review. The post should include: combined statement total ($21,940), Q1 disputed amount ($4,800), April rate inflation (contracted $1,600 vs billed $2,270-$2,310 per move), affected accounts below 25% margin floor (GreenStack 23.4%, NorthWind 21.8%, Mosaic 17.1%), estimated $18.6K contribution shortfall, and the active booking freeze risk.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails for Heartland Movers correspondence to find Jake Loomis's combined statement email (subject: "Revised Statement and Payment Request - HM-2026-Q1-0042 + April Services") sent to hana.kim@moveops.com, CC marcus.thorne@moveops.com. The agent discovers the combined balance due is $21,940: Q1 invoice HM-2026-Q1-0042 at $12,800 (outstanding, 45+ days past due) plus April services HM-2026-APR-1187 totaling $9,140 - GreenStack Energy 2 relocations at $2,280 each ($4,560), Mosaic Robotics 1 relocation at $2,310, Canopy Health 1 relocation at $2,270.
OE 2: Search emails for the Q1 dispute history to find Marcus Thorne's formal dispute letter to Jake Loomis and Chloe Vance's analysis email identifying the $4,800 discrepancy (3 of 8 moves invalid). The agent discovers: 5 legitimate Q1 moves at $1,600 each = $8,000 (Abdi, Tran, Kovac, Webb, Reeves), 3 disputed at $1,600 each = $4,800 (Lily Marchetti - cancelled Feb 10, Danielle Osei - cancelled Feb 13, Priya Venkatesh - handled by Swift Relocations not Heartland). Marcus sent the formal dispute on ~March 25 but no corrected invoice has been received from Heartland.
OE 3: Search Airtable relocation records (tblRelocations01) to verify which moves Heartland actually performed in April and cross-reference each of the 4 line items from Jake's statement. The agent discovers the contracted vendor cost is $1,600 per move, confirming Jake's April rates ($2,270-$2,310) represent a 42-44% increase. The agent also identifies the coordinator assigned to each move for later notification.
OE 4: Search CRM deal records for each client on Jake's April statement (GreenStack Energy, Mosaic Robotics, Canopy Health) to pull contract values and compute per-client margin exposure. The agent discovers deal records including deal amounts (e.g., deal_canopy_webb_apr2026 at $3,200, deal_mosaic_okoye_apr2026_risk at $2,700) enabling comparison of what MoveOps charges clients versus what Heartland is billing, giving per-client margin impact rather than just aggregate exposure.
OE 5: Search QuickBooks for Heartland Movers bills to find the current financial state. The agent discovers: (a) bill_heartland_q1_2026_hold ($12,800, DocNumber HM-2026-Q1-0042-HOLD) with 5 lines marked [VERIFIED] and 3 marked DISPUTED (Marchetti, Osei, Venkatesh), confirming the payment hold is still active and untouched; (b) bill_heartland_q4_2025 ($6,400, Balance $0, fully paid) for 4 moves at $1,600 each, confirming Q4 was clean and the pricing issue started in Q1.
OE 6: Search Linear for the Heartland dispute tracking ticket to find issue_heartland_q1_recon (title: "Vendor invoice reconciliation - Heartland Movers Q1 2026 (HM-2026-Q1-0042)"), number 342, assigned to Chloe Vance, state: in_progress. The agent reads the checklist and discovers that tracking Heartland's response, receiving corrected invoice, and releasing payment hold are all still incomplete. The agent also reads Chloe's comment confirming Q4 2025 was clean and this appears to be a process gap on Heartland's end.
OE 7: Search emails for Jake Loomis's rate increase notices to understand the scope of the pricing pressure. The agent discovers Jake's formal notice of revised pricing on the MoveOps account effective for new bookings beginning April 20, 2026, with adjusted rates tied to the unresolved Q1 dispute. This explains why the April charges ($2,270-$2,310) exceed the contracted $1,600 rate.
OE 8: Search Slack messages in the #operations channel (C006) for the latest on Heartland scheduling status. The agent discovers Chloe Vance's message reporting that Heartland will not accept any new MoveOps relocation bookings until the Q1 billing dispute is resolved. The message names current April relocations on Heartland's board: Mosaic Robotics, GreenStack Energy, and Canopy Health, and notes that active jobs are still scheduled but new or add-on bookings are at risk of rejection.
OE 9: Search Airtable relocation records for all active (In Progress) relocations involving Heartland Movers as a vendor to identify which client relocations and coordinators would be affected by a booking freeze. The agent discovers Heartland-dependent active relocations including: Marcus Webb / Canopy Health (Detroit to Chicago, split-vendor with Heartland for household goods, coordinator Blessing Okafor), Emilia Cruz / NorthWind Technologies (Chicago to Denver, Heartland for household goods, coordinator Blessing Okafor), Terrence Nakamura / Vectral Systems (Austin to San Francisco, split shipment with Heartland for ~6,000 lbs household goods, coordinator Suki Patel), Tyler Okonkwo / Canopy Health (Philadelphia to Denver, vendor TBD - Heartland or Swift, coordinator Javier Morales), and Joaquin Reyes / GreenStack Energy (Phoenix to Seattle, Heartland initially planned for oversize rig transport, coordinator Javier Morales).
OE 10: Search Airtable Client Accounts table or CRM to identify account managers for clients with Heartland-dependent relocations. The agent discovers: Canopy Health - Catalina Dubois, NorthWind Technologies - Catalina Dubois, Vectral Systems - Mina Hashimoto, GreenStack Energy - Emeka Diallo, Mosaic Robotics - Emeka Diallo.
OE 11: Search emails or Slack for Alejandro Fuentes's margin impact analysis. The agent discovers Alejandro's email (subject: "Margin downside view if Heartland and Swift increases both hit April volume") sent to marcus.thorne@moveops.com and hana.kim@moveops.com showing three accounts fall below the 25% margin floor: GreenStack Energy at 23.4%, NorthWind Technologies at 21.8%, and Mosaic Robotics at 17.1%, with approximately $18.6K in lost contribution versus plan.
OE 12: Look up contact email addresses for Jake Loomis (jake.loomis@heartlandmovers.com), the account managers - Catalina Dubois (catalina.dubois@moveops.com), Emeka Diallo (emeka.diallo@moveops.com), Mina Hashimoto (mina.hashimoto@moveops.com) - and the coordinators on active Heartland moves - Blessing Okafor (blessing.okafor@moveops.com), Javier Morales (javier.morales@moveops.com), Suki Patel (suki.patel@moveops.com).
OE 13: Search Slack channels to identify the #finance channel (channel_id: C005) for the finance breakdown post, and to find existing Slack discussions about the Heartland dispute for additional context.
OE 14: Send an email from hana.kim@moveops.com to jake.loomis@heartlandmovers.com laying out MoveOps' position: (a) the Q1 disputed items - reiterating that 3 of 8 line items on HM-2026-Q1-0042 are invalid ($4,800 in dispute: Marchetti cancelled, Osei cancelled, Venkatesh handled by Swift), and that MoveOps will only pay $8,000 for the 5 verified moves; (b) the unapproved April rates - rejecting the $2,270-$2,310 per-move rates as unauthorized increases over the contracted $1,600 rate, and requesting corrected April invoicing at the agreed rate.
OE 15: Search QuickBooks for the existing Q1 hold bill (quickbooks_search_bills or quickbooks_get_bill for bill_heartland_q1_2026_hold) to confirm it remains active and untouched. Then create a new bill (quickbooks_create_bill) for the April charges separating the undisputed amounts at contracted rates ($1,600 per move) from the contested rate inflation ($2,270-$2,310 billed vs $1,600 contracted per move), so MoveOps has a clear record of what is owed versus what is disputed for the April period.
OE 16: Update the dispute tracking ticket in Linear (via linear_create_comment on issue_heartland_q1_recon) with the latest combined financial exposure: the dispute has expanded from the original $4,800 Q1 overbilling to a combined $21,940 demand including April rate inflation, with Heartland threatening booking freezes affecting active client relocations.
OE 17: Send separate emails from hana.kim@moveops.com to each affected account manager with their specific clients' exposure and freeze risk: (a) emeka.diallo@moveops.com - GreenStack Energy and Mosaic Robotics exposure, Heartland-dependent moves for those clients, margin impact (GreenStack 23.4%, Mosaic 17.1%); (b) catalina.dubois@moveops.com - Canopy Health and NorthWind Technologies exposure, Heartland-dependent moves (Webb, Okonkwo, Cruz), margin impact (NorthWind 21.8%); (c) mina.hashimoto@moveops.com - Vectral Systems exposure, Nakamura split-shipment dependency on Heartland.
OE 18: Send messages to the coordinators actively managing Heartland-dependent moves so they can flag scheduling changes immediately: Blessing Okafor (managing Webb/Canopy and Cruz/NorthWind moves via Heartland), Javier Morales (managing Okonkwo/Canopy and Reyes/GreenStack with Heartland involvement), and Suki Patel (managing Nakamura/Vectral split-shipment with Heartland for household goods).
OE 19: Flag at-risk CRM deals by using crm_update_deal_stage to change deal stages to at_risk for deals associated with GreenStack Energy, Mosaic Robotics, Canopy Health, NorthWind Technologies, and Vectral Systems, and using crm_create_engagement on each company to log notes documenting the billing dispute, unapproved rate increases, and potential booking freeze impact on active relocations.
OE 20: Post a message to the #finance Slack channel (C005) with a breakdown of total disputed amount and margin impact for Marcus Thorne and Alejandro Fuentes' visibility before the vendor strategy review. The post should include: combined statement total ($21,940), Q1 disputed amount ($4,800), April rate inflation (contracted $1,600 vs billed $2,270-$2,310 per move), affected accounts below 25% margin floor (GreenStack 23.4%, NorthWind 21.8%, Mosaic 17.1%), estimated $18.6K contribution shortfall, and the active booking freeze risk.
📋 Rubrics
Rubric Results

# Criterion Run #1Run #2Run #3Run #4Run #5Run #6 Pass Rate
1 An email was sent (via send_email or reply_to_email) from [hana.kim@moveops.com](mailto:hana.kim@moveops.com) to [jake.loomis@heartlandmovers.com](mailto:jake.loomis@heartlandmovers.com).
outcome
 —✓✓✓✓— 100%
—Run #1No justification
✓Run #2The agent called reply_to_email with sender 'hana.kim@moveops.com' on email_id 'email_heartland_combined_invoice_apr_q1_demand', which is Jake Loomis's combined statement email. The reply was successfully sent.
✓Run #3The agent called reply_to_email with sender 'hana.kim@moveops.com' and target email 'email_heartland_combined_invoice_apr_q1_demand', which is Jake Loomis's combined statement email at jake.loomis@heartlandmovers.com. The tool returned a successful email_id.

outcome

✓Run #3The reply email explicitly states: 'The rates applied — $2,270 to $2,310 per relocation — are significantly above our contracted rate of $1,600 per move' and 'MoveOps did not agree to revised pricing', clearly rejecting the April rate increases as unauthorized.
✓Run #4The email to Jake states: 'Our contracted rate with Heartland is $1,600 per full-service relocation... The April statement bills at $2,270–$2,310 per move, representing a 42–44% increase over contracted pricing.' It explicitly disputes the unauthorized rate increases.
✓Run #5The reply_to_email content clearly rejects the April rates: GreenStack billed $2,280/move, Mosaic at $2,310/move, Canopy at $2,270/move versus the contracted $1,600/move, stating 'MoveOps never agreed to revised pricing' and that the $2,740 difference 'represents an unapproved unilateral rate increase that we do not accept.'

query construction

✗Run #4All airtable_search_records calls used company names (GreenStack, Mosaic, Canopy) as the search value in the 'Company' field. None of the Airtable searches used 'Heartland' or 'Heartland Movers' as a search value to find relocation records by vendor name.
